banner.title: Wan 2.6 AI Video Generator
banner.description: Wan 2.6 is Alibaba's latest Wan AI video model for cinematic multi-shot creation. It supports text-to-video, image-to-video, and reference-guided generation with up to 15-second 1080p output, native audio sync, and stronger temporal consistency.

tabs.title: Text to Video
tabs.subTitle: Prompt
tabs.placeholder: Describe your multi-shot scene, camera moves, and mood...

tabs.title: Image to Video
tabs.subTitle: Image
tabs.placeholder: Upload an image to animate with Wan 2.6
tabs.tips: Upload JPG/PNG/WEBP images up to 10MB, minimum width/height of 300px.

details.title: Holistic Visual Reference for Starring Workflows
details.description: Wan's official introduction highlights Starring for casting characters from reference videos into new scenes. With up to 150 reference frames, Wan 2.6 preserves appearance and audio consistency with high accuracy.

details.title: Precise Timbre Preservation
details.description: Wan 2.6 can faithfully replicate character timbre from reference videos and keep multimodal consistency across sight and sound, improving voice continuity for character-driven stories.

details.title: Multi-Character Interaction
details.description: Wan 2.6 supports up to three simultaneous references, enabling multiple entities to co-star in one coherent sequence while maintaining identity and style continuity across shots.

details.title: Intelligent Multi-Shot Narrative
details.description: Wan's official page describes intelligent multi-shot narrative generation: simple prompts can be expanded into shot-by-shot storyboards while keeping characters, scenes, and mood consistent for richer storytelling.

details.title: 15s 1080p Cinematic Output with Native Audio-Video Sync
details.description: Both Wan and Kie pages describe cinematic-quality generation up to 15 seconds at 1080p, with stable multi-shot transitions, synchronized native audio, and stronger camera motion control.

details.title: Text, Image, and Reference-to-Video Modes
details.description: Wan 2.6 supports T2V, I2V, and reference-to-video workflows. It translates storyboard-like prompts into coherent sequences, animates still images with identity retention, and transfers appearance and style cues from reference clips.

usageGuide.title: How to Use Wan 2.6
usageGuide.description: Create cinematic AI videos with Wan 2.6 in a few steps

steps.title: Choose Wan 2.6 and Generation Mode
steps.description: Select Wan 2.6, then choose text-to-video, image-to-video, or reference-guided workflow based on your source material.

steps.title: Provide Prompt and Inputs
steps.description: Write a structured prompt with shots and camera directions, and upload an image or reference clip when needed. Set duration and resolution options for your target output.

steps.title: Generate, Review, and Download
steps.description: Run generation and review continuity, audio sync, and motion pacing. Iterate your prompt or references, then download the final video.

faq.name: faq
faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Learn more about Wan 2.6 features and workflows

items.title: What is Wan 2.6?
items.description: Wan 2.6 is the latest Wan AI video model from Alibaba, focused on multi-shot cinematic generation with stronger scene continuity, more stable character identity, and native audio-video synchronization.

items.title: What generation modes does Wan 2.6 support?
items.description: Wan 2.6 supports text-to-video (T2V), image-to-video (I2V), and reference-to-video (R2V) workflows, so you can generate from prompts, still images, or reference clips.

items.title: What output quality can I expect from Wan 2.6?
items.description: Wan 2.6 can generate up to 15-second 1080p videos with smoother multi-shot transitions, better temporal consistency, and synchronized native audio.

items.title: How does Wan 2.6 improve character consistency?
items.description: Wan 2.6 improves shot-to-shot identity stability and supports reference-guided generation, helping preserve faces, wardrobe, textures, and style across scenes.

items.title: What is Starring in Wan 2.6?
items.description: Wan's official introduction describes Starring as casting characters from reference videos into new scenes, with holistic visual reference, timbre preservation, and multi-character interaction support.

items.title: Does Wan 2.6 support native audio?
items.description: Yes. Wan 2.6 combines visual generation with synchronized native audio, including dialogue and ambient sound alignment, to produce more production-ready video outputs.

items.title: Is Wan 2.6 good for multi-shot storytelling?
items.description: Yes. Wan 2.6 is designed for storyboard-like prompts and can maintain pacing, mood, and scene coherence across multiple shots for more cinematic storytelling.
